intro: Onoseris altissima, commonly known as the "Peruvian Thistle," is a species of plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the highlands of Peru. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Onoseris altissima include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues and as a natural remedy for various conditions.
